findings.description: Agenda content rendered as a table in HTML instead of the original list-style agenda layout.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the agenda is presented as aligned time/topic/speaker lines with dot leaders and sub-items beneath entries. In the HTML, this same content is converted into a bordered 3-column table with header row ('Time', 'Topic', 'Speaker'), changing the visual structure substantially.

findings.description: Heading alignment and emphasis differ from the PDF.
findings.evidence: The PDF centers the main title block with bold hierarchy, while the HTML left-aligns the title and meeting details. The textual content is preserved, but the visual distinction and alignment are altered.

findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated metadata and styling text not present in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML contains '<title>Converted Document</title>', CSS comments/text such as 'Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS', '@import url(...)', and theme variable names; the PDF only shows the meeting agenda text and does not contain these words.

findings.description: The HTML does not use semantic heading elements for the document title and subtitle structure.
findings.evidence:
PDF visually presents title lines 'French Broad Hydrologic Model' and 'Stakeholder Meeting #2' as headings. HTML encodes them inside a

...

block rather than h1/h2 elements.
